Title: Identification and functional analysis of two P450 enzymes of Gossypium hirsutum involved in DMNT and TMTT biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for metabolic engineering or transgenic enhancement of cotton indirect defense by increasing DMNT and/or TMTT emission to attract natural enemies of herbivores; likely best deployed with upstream terpene synthases that provide (E)-nerolidol and (E,E)-geranyllinalool, and with caution for ecological context-dependent effects on volatile blends.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively degrades (E)-nerolidol to DMNT and (E,E)-geranyllinalool to TMTT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the homoterpenes DMNT and TMTT in cotton, especially under herbivore attack.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; enzyme activity assay with authentic substrates; GC-MS/SPME-GC-MS product identification; qPCR expression profiling in damaged versus control plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Herbivore damage by Helicoverpa armigera or Apolygus lucorum for 24 h strongly induced expression and volatile emission.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest transcript abundance in stems, moderate in leaves, trace in roots; significantly up-regulated in leaves and stems after H. armigera or A. lucorum attack.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in stems, moderate in leaves, trace in roots; induced by herbivore attack.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previous transcriptome data of G. hirsutum infested with A. lucorum were used for candidate screening; accession NA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from candidate gene CotAD_50571; two target CYPs are highly similar allelic sequences within the CYP82L family; activity required CPR co-expression in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth or yield penalty reported; engineering altered volatile blends may have ecology-dependent effects on insect interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP82L family; contains conserved proline-rich, PERF, heme-binding and substrate recognition domains.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP82L1 was identified as one of the only tested cotton P450s able to convert (E)-nerolidol to DMNT and (E,E)-geranyllinalool to TMTT in yeast assays. 2. Its catalytic activity depended on co-expression with CPR. 3. Its transcript level was strongly induced in leaves and stems of herbivore-damaged cotton, linking the gene to induced homoterpene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Emitted from damaged aerial tissues, especially leaves/stems of cotton plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect defense signals that attract parasitoid natural enemies of herbivores.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering enhanced cotton indirect defense via DMNT/TMTT emission; may be stacked with upstream terpene synthases and inducible defense systems to improve parasitoid attraction while minimizing unnecessary volatile release.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively degrades (E)-nerolidol to DMNT and (E,E)-geranyllinalool to TMTT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the homoterpenes DMNT and TMTT in cotton, particularly in herbivore-induced tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; enzyme activity assay with authentic substrates; GC-MS/SPME-GC-MS product identification; qPCR expression profiling in damaged versus control plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Herbivore damage by Helicoverpa armigera or Apolygus lucorum for 24 h strongly induced expression and volatile emission.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest transcript abundance in stems, moderate in leaves, trace in roots; significantly up-regulated in leaves and stems after H. armigera or A. lucorum attack.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in stems, moderate in leaves, trace in roots; induced by herbivore attack.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previous transcriptome data of G. hirsutum infested with A. lucorum were used for candidate screening; accession NA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from candidate gene CotAD_50571 as a second highly similar allele-like sequence; shares 95.34% identity with GhCYP82L1; activity required CPR co-expression in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth or yield penalty reported; engineering altered volatile blends may have ecology-dependent effects on insect interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP82L family; contains conserved proline-rich, PERF, heme-binding and substrate recognition domains.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP82L2 was functionally validated as a cotton P450 converting (E)-nerolidol to DMNT and (E,E)-geranyllinalool to TMTT in yeast assays. 2. Enzyme function required co-expression with CPR. 3. GhCYP82L2 transcripts accumulated mainly in stems and leaves and were strongly induced by herbivore feeding, supporting its role in induced homoterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Emitted from damaged aerial tissues, especially leaves/stems of cotton plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect defense signals that attract parasitoid natural enemies of herbivores.
